gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","6 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"January 18, 2025"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-6 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"More screens doesn't always mean more productivity. Here's how to choose the monitor setup that actually matches your work."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-multi-monitor-myth","children":"The Multi-Monitor Myth"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"There's a widespread belief that more monitors automatically equals more productivity. Research suggests the reality is more complicated."}}],"$L29","$L2a","$L2b","$L2c","$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36","$L37","$L38","$L39","$L3a","$L3b","$L3c","$L3d","$L3e"]}],"$L3f","$L40","$L41"]}],"$L42"]}]]}]
43:I[94097,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
44:I[24170,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
45:I[55132,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505740420928-5e560c06d30e?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1524758631624-e2822e304c36?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
29:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Adding a second monitor does measurably improve productivity for tasks involving frequent reference switching — comparing documents, coding with documentation open, monitoring dashboards. For tasks requiring sustained focus on a single thing, multiple monitors are often a distraction source."}}]
2a:["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The right setup depends on what you actually do."}}]
2b:["$","h2","4",{"id":"the-single-monitor-case","children":"The Single Monitor Case"}]
2c:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A single large, high-quality monitor in the 27–32\" range (ideally 4K) is underrated. It forces window discipline, reduces the temptation to multitask, and results in a cleaner, calmer desk."}}]
2d:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For writers, designers doing focused work, and anyone who finds dual monitors pulls their attention, this is often the best setup."}}]
2e:["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Best options</strong>: LG 27UK850-W (27\" 4K USB-C), Dell U2722D (27\" QHD), Samsung ViewFinity S8 (27\" 4K)."}}]
2f:["$","h2","8",{"id":"the-dual-monitor-case","children":"The Dual Monitor Case"}]
30:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Two monitors work best when your work genuinely requires two parallel contexts — a primary working space and a reference/communication space."}}]
31:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The key is intentionality. Designate what lives on each monitor. Email and Slack live on the secondary monitor; work lives on the primary. Without that discipline, the second monitor becomes a distraction machine."}}]
32:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For the secondary monitor, you don't need top specs — a simple 24\" 1080p panel works fine."}}]
33:["$","h2","12",{"id":"the-ultrawide-case","children":"The Ultrawide Case"}]
34:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A 34\" or 38\" ultrawide (21:9 or 16:10 aspect ratio) gives you the screen real estate of dual monitors without the bezel in the middle of your view."}}]
35:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The lack of a seam is genuinely pleasant for creative work. The wide aspect ratio is excellent for video editing, photo editing, and coding."}}]
36:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Downsides: they're expensive, they require a GPU that can drive them, and some applications don't handle ultra-wide aspect ratios gracefully."}}]
37:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Best options</strong>: LG 34WN780 (34\" QHD), Dell U3423WE (34\" QHD with USB-C hub), Samsung Odyssey G9 (49\" if you want to go truly extreme)."}}]
38:["$","h2","17",{"id":"panel-technology","children":"Panel Technology"}]
39:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>IPS</strong>: Best colors and viewing angles. Slight glow in dark scenes. The right choice for most people."}}]
3a:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>VA</strong>: Excellent contrast and deep blacks. Slightly slower response than IPS. Good for multimedia consumption and mixed use."}}]
3b:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>OLED</strong>: Stunning picture quality, perfect blacks, expensive, possible burn-in with static content. Best for creative professionals and premium builds."}}]
3c:["$","h2","21",{"id":"resolution-and-refresh-rate","children":"Resolution and Refresh Rate"}]
3d:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For a desk monitor, 1440p (QHD) is the sweet spot — noticeably sharper than 1080p, less GPU-demanding than 4K. At 27\", 1440p looks excellent."}}]
3e:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For monitors used primarily for productivity (not gaming), a 60Hz refresh rate is perfectly adequate. The difference between 60Hz and 144Hz is much more noticeable in fast-paced games than in a text editor."}}]
3f:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Gear","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-orange-50 text-orange-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Gear"}],[["$","$L7","monitors",{"href":"/blog/tag/monitors","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","monitors"]}],["$","$L7","display",{"href":"/blog/tag/display","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","display"]}],["$","$L7","dual-monitor",{"href":"/blog/tag/dual-monitor","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","dual-monitor"]}],["$","$L7","peripherals",{"href":"/blog/tag/peripherals","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","peripherals"]}]]]}]
40:["$","$L43",null,{"title":"Single vs Dual vs Ultrawide: The Monitor Setup Guide","slug":"monitor-setup-guide"}]
41:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L44",null,{}]}]
42:["$","aside",null,{"className":"space-y-5 print:hidden","children":[["$","$L45",null,{"items":[{"id":"the-multi-monitor-myth","text":"The Multi-Monitor Myth","level":2},{"id":"the-single-monitor-case","text":"The Single Monitor Case","level":2},{"id":"the-dual-monitor-case","text":"The Dual Monitor Case","level":2},{"id":"the-ultrawide-case","text":"The Ultrawide Case","level":2},{"id":"panel-technology","text":"Panel Technology","level":2},{"id":"resolution-and-refresh-rate","text":"Resolution and Refresh Rate","level":2}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108377-be9c29b29330?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Mia Collins","className":"w-14 h-14 rounded-2xl object-cover object-top mb-4"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-lg text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 mb-0.5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Mia Collins"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-orange-500 font-medium mb-3","children":"Workspace Designer"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"A decade designing ergonomic workspaces for remote teams. 47:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/01xlw8hd842-c.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"IconMark"]
